trivial-fixes.name: trivial-fixes
trivial-fixes.description: Zero-judgment mechanical fixes. For each item: read the issue detail, apply the fix, verify with tsc.
trivial-fixes.action: Fix each item, verify with tsc --noEmit
action_steps: Focus: desloppify plan focus trivial-fixes
Unused imports: delete the import line
Stray logs: delete the console.log line
Import cycles: break by extracting shared types to a new types.ts file
Hardcoded URLs: these are in TEST files — extract to a TEST_FIXTURES constant at top of test file
Voided symbols: delete the dead code (unused variable/function)
Console errors without throw: add throw after console.error, or convert to proper error handling
Window globals: wrap in typeof window check or move to debug module
Verify: npx tsc --noEmit
Resolve each: desloppify plan resolve <id> --note <what you did> --confirm

async-discipline.name: async-discipline
async-discipline.description: INVESTIGATION RESULT: Almost all 36 items are false positives — the functions already await all their async work correctly. The linter flags them because the outer function signature says async but never uses a bare await at the top level (it awaits inside try/catch, conditionals, etc). The fix for ~33 of 36 items is: REMOVE THE ASYNC KEYWORD if the function returns a Promise directly, OR verify the function genuinely needs async. Only 3 items need ADD VOID (test file + migration effect).
async-discipline.action: Read each function. Don't blanket void everything.
action_steps: Focus: desloppify plan focus async-discipline
For each item: read 10 lines around the flagged line
Check: does the function body actually await anything? If yes, async is correct — the item is a false positive, resolve with note 'async is correct, all code paths await'
If function returns a Promise directly without await, REMOVE ASYNC and let it return Promise naturally
Exception: submitSegmentTask.test.ts lines 72 and 114 — add void wrapper
Exception: useLoraCategoryMigration.ts line 30 — already uses void, verify and resolve
Verify: npx tsc --noEmit after each batch of ~5 files
Resolve each: desloppify plan resolve <id> --note <action taken> --confirm

detail.description: Fix 36 async functions that don't await. IMPORTANT: Read each one individually. Do NOT blanket-fix by adding void everywhere. Some are real bugs (missing error handling on mutations/uploads). Some are intentional fire-and-forget.

3. If intentional fire-and-forget: add void prefix to signal intent
4. If bug (missing error handling on mutation/upload/fetch): add await or .catch() with proper error handling
5. If the async keyword itself is unnecessary (no awaits inside): remove async keyword entirely
6. SKIP items in files that are flagged for structural decomposition (struct-* clusters) — those will be rewritten anyway
7. After all: npx tsc --noEmit, then desloppify scan --path .

detail.description: Three related dimensions: API coherence (48.5%), Init coupling (50.4%), Error consistency (58.2%). Cache keys are inconsistent, auth state leaks on signout, errors are swallowed.
steps: 1. Cache key unification (2 issues): find all query key definitions for generation-task mapping. Pick ONE pattern. Update all consumers. Test with React Query devtools that invalidation still works.
2. Stale auth cache on signout: find the tool settings cache. Add a listener on auth state change that clears it. Test: sign out, sign in as different user, verify no stale data.
3. Join clips localStorage queue: replace ambient localStorage queue with explicit state passing. The consumer should not depend on timing of when localStorage was written.
4. Realtime singleton: make the connection lazy-initialized (not on import). Constructor should not have side effects.
5. Optimistic update failure: when the optimistic update's mutation fails, roll back the optimistic state. Don't persist the optimistic value as truth.
6. Task details error handling: when task details fetch fails, show an error state, not an empty state. Users need to know something went wrong.
7. After: desloppify scan --path . and check all 3 dimension scores

detail.description: Zero-judgment mechanical fixes. For each item: read the issue detail, apply the fix, verify with tsc.
steps: Focus: desloppify plan focus trivial-fixes
Unused imports: delete the import line
Stray logs: delete the console.log line
Import cycles: break by extracting shared types to a new types.ts file
Hardcoded URLs: these are in TEST files — extract to a TEST_FIXTURES constant at top of test file
Voided symbols: delete the dead code (unused variable/function)
Console errors without throw: add throw after console.error, or convert to proper error handling
Window globals: wrap in typeof window check or move to debug module
Verify: npx tsc --noEmit
Resolve each: desloppify plan resolve <id> --note <what you did> --confirm

detail.description: INVESTIGATION RESULT: Almost all 36 items are false positives — the functions already await all their async work correctly. The linter flags them because the outer function signature says async but never uses a bare await at the top level (it awaits inside try/catch, conditionals, etc). The fix for ~33 of 36 items is: REMOVE THE ASYNC KEYWORD if the function returns a Promise directly, OR verify the function genuinely needs async. Only 3 items need ADD VOID (test file + migration effect).
steps: Focus: desloppify plan focus async-discipline
For each item: read 10 lines around the flagged line
Check: does the function body actually await anything? If yes, async is correct — the item is a false positive, resolve with note 'async is correct, all code paths await'
If function returns a Promise directly without await, REMOVE ASYNC and let it return Promise naturally
Exception: submitSegmentTask.test.ts lines 72 and 114 — add void wrapper
Exception: useLoraCategoryMigration.ts line 30 — already uses void, verify and resolve
Verify: npx tsc --noEmit after each batch of ~5 files
Resolve each: desloppify plan resolve <id> --note <action taken> --confirm
